content:
Let me cut to the chase.

If you have a third party antivirus product that purports ot do anything with mail especially look out foe spam and scams or it has toolbars in Thunderbird or it puts buttons in Thunderbird then there is a 99% chance that is the root cause of your problem.

Folk appear here periodically complaining of thyese spam sorting issues. Most never make any sort of reply as o if they found a solution, so I assume you the information they were given fixed their problem. So this topic has a number of see also links to the side.

The first is. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1513371

Automatic assumption that thunderbird was broken. No reply to the initial offer of information and that is fairly typical. It is difficult to get a handle in exactly the issue when no one actually replies with what their issue really was. I suspect many just give up. They want someone to waive the log into their system and fix it solution their ISP uses and fail to accept any other approach.

I recall years ago someone identified Trend and it's toolbar, but I just suspect all antivirus as it is unnecessary for email (I use defender and it has no mail functions) and generally of questionable quality. It does not have to be good if it crashes no one knows.

This is the trend feature I am talking about https://helpcenter.trendmicro.com/en-us/article/tmka-10500

content:
Why you would think things disappearing on your system for synchronization is different from other folks experience with synchronization I have no idea. That is explicitly how IMAP works,

Most of the queries I see about disappearing mail are people using IMAP and then come here because they do not understand synchronize means what happens on the server is replicates locally. So they want to ask why a syncronised services synchronizes and their mail disappears, I see your posting as just another of these.

Your provider undoubtedly deletes SPAM email after a set time. (google I think have a 14 day retention on things in that folder.) Thunderbird by default makes no attempt to download the mail in the spam folder or trash for that matter and synchronize it locally unless you explicitly select the folder to view. When you select the folder, the mail synchronizes and the older garbage is syncronised into oblivion per your providers policy.

You can right click the folder, select properties and set the folder to be included in get mail actions so it is always up to date, but if you are going to do that you probably should do the trash/deleted folder as well as those two folders (Junk and trash) are explicitly not updated unless you select them. Changing the synchronization setting makes no real change to what happens, it will just be you will no longer see it when it happens like you can now.

Asssuming IMAP, my approach would be to verify that the synchronization setting was for all messsages regardless of age and then

  1. click file>offlline>download
  2. when done, click help>troubleshootinginformation
  3. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'

content:
There are a load if issues here. and you and not going to like what your hear at all.

Yahoo apparent have a hard limit of 10,00 email per IMAP folder. They just do not synchronise them after that. They do offer another server, that is said to offer only one way downloads, but documentation of that is sparse to say the least.

Yahoo also have a limit on the daily bandwidth you can use for IMAP or POP. It is a couple og gigabytes and most of those desperate to get their mail off Yahoo have significantly more mail than that to get. SO a full synchronization might take weeks. Depending on how much data is involved.

Yahoo observer "security through obscurity" and as such do not document limits they impose on their systems like the number of concurrent connections but anecdotal evidence is that 'Yahoo impose a limit of five connections this is fine for most IMAP uses, but falls over rather quickly if you have apple products (the have no governor facility in their settings. Each device will consume all available connections or if you have multiple devices accessing the account like say a phone, tablet and Thunderbird. You run out of available connections quite quickly even if each device is quite moderate with its demands. SO good practice is to have nothing else accessing that mail account while you try and download it.

IMAP downloads are not stable archival downloads, hence Davids advice to move the data as soon as it is downloaded. IMAP use at best a type of cache of what the server holds. If mail disappear from the server (are removed or deleted) IMAP says they will also disappear from the local storage as it is synchronized to the server

Note hat Thunderbird will download message headers first, then the message bodies including attachments. This makes a list appear in Thunderbird of messages in a folder relatively quickly, but at the time the list appears the downloads are rarely even close to complete with a heavy lifting still continuing in the background. I suggest you have a look at the activity manager (double clicking the status bar at the bottom of the Thunderbird window will show it) It will show the status of most of your mail fetch and indexing processes. It will also give information on connection failures and dropped connections. Just be aware you may have to drag the dialog quite wide to see the full text of each item. It does not wrap messages

content:
From what I can find on the Internet, this is a "friendly-fied" version of email server error number 5.7.1 . It indicates that recipient server has rejected a message because the sending server is misconfigured. An AI summary of the situation is...

"The SMTP 550 5.7.1 error indicates that the recipient's server rejected your email due to security, policy, or authentication issues, such as a blocked IP, missing DNS records (SPF/DKIM), or missing SMTP authentication. The most effective immediate fixes are ensuring "SMTP Authentication" is enabled in your email client settings and verifying that your email domain is properly authenticated."

Google "email error 5.7.1" for more info.,

I believe that you the user cannot directly do anything about it except to report it to your email administrator. The only thing you can do on your end that might help is confirm that your SMTP server settings indicate the proper Authentication Method,

content:
Please give careful details. I suggest you post screenshot of your old profile. The profiles.ini text file in the old appdata\roaming\thunderbird folder will name the profile in the appdata\roaming\thunderbird\profiles folder. From there, click to the mail\Local Folders folder and do screenshot of that.

On the recovery steps, do in this order:

  1. if there is an appdata\roaming\thunderbird folder on the drive, delete it
  2. install thunderbird, start once and exit. This establishes a base pointer
  3. now, delete the roaming\thunderbird folder
  4. then, paste your old thunderbird folder there
  5. now, restart thunderbird and data should be there. If not, seeing the requested screenshot is important to give further advice. thank you
